title: I used claude to make a program that tests the probability of significance for support and resistance levels.
author: Posterior_Mind
selftext:
I’ve been building an AI-assisted probabilistic trading framework that evaluates thousands of potential market levels simultaneously using Bayesian analysis and posterior predictive modeling.

Instead of manually drawing support/resistance zones, the system scans large sets of possible price interaction levels and assigns probabilities for potential significance based on evolving market conditions, volatility structure, and historical behavioral patterns.

The interesting part has been watching how often price reacts near the highest probability posterior predictive levels (PPLs), especially in:

* BTC
* IWM
* leveraged ETFs
* volatility products

A few things I’ve noticed:

* volatility expansion frequently occurs between high-confidence regions
* reversals often cluster near posterior extremes
* some lower probability levels get ignored entirely while higher probability regions repeatedly attract price interaction

The AI component has been useful because it can continuously evaluate and reprioritize thousands of candidate levels in real time — far beyond what would realistically be possible manually.

Curious if anyone else here is applying AI or Bayesian inference to probabilistic price mapping rather than standard indicator-based systems.
